acceptanceCriteria.description: User can ask 'select 10 for an exhibit' in Phase 2 and receive a formatted list of 10 items with significance notes, instead of the current 'not yet available' message.
acceptanceCriteria.verification: Manual test via /chat endpoint with a session in CORPUS_EXPLORATION phase.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Selected items are diverse: for a 120-record subgroup spanning 5 decades and 8 places, the 10 selected items cover at least 3 decades and 3 places.
Each selected item includes a human-readable significance note explaining why it was chosen (e.g., 'Early printing (1475)', 'Rich enrichment data').
The curation engine works without E4's thematic_context module, falling back to basic heuristic scoring.

Exploration agent correctly classifies curation requests: 'select 10 for exhibit', 'most important items', 'representative sample', 'rarest books', 'diverse selection' all map to RECOMMENDATION intent.
acceptanceCriteria.verification: System prompt update with 5 explicit examples. LLM classification test (integration, requires API key).

acceptanceCriteria.description: Exhibit response includes dimension coverage summary: 'Coverage: 5 decades, 4 places, 3 languages, 6 agents.'
User can specify the number of items to select: 'pick 5', 'select 20 items', 'top 15'. recommendation_count is extracted by the LLM and clamped to [1, 50].
acceptanceCriteria.verification: Schema test for ExplorationRequestLLM.recommendation_count field. Handler clamp test.

acceptanceCriteria.description: All 25 new tests pass. No regressions in existing test suite.
Q20 score improves from 1/25 to at least 12/25: the 120-record curated exhibit query now returns a meaningful, ranked, diverse selection with significance explanations.
acceptanceCriteria.verification: Manual evaluation against Q20 rubric after implementation.

affectedComponents.description: Core curation module: scoring, diverse selection, and exhibit formatting. Contains ScoredCandidate model, score_candidates(), select_diverse(), and format_exhibit().
Add recommendation_count and recommendation_criteria fields to ExplorationRequestLLM schema. Update system prompt with RECOMMENDATION examples showing curation-style requests.
Replace static RECOMMENDATION stub (lines 1025-1038) with working handler that calls curator.score_candidates() and curator.select_diverse(), then formats exhibit response.
Add format_exhibit_item() and format_exhibit_response() functions for exhibit-style display with significance notes.
ExplorationIntent.RECOMMENDATION already exists. ChatResponse.metadata dict can carry curation metadata without schema changes.

E4 defines significance_score() with factors: date_rarity, enrichment_richness, place_rarity, subject_richness. E5 imports and reuses this function. If E4 is not yet implemented, curator.py includes a minimal fallback stub that returns a flat 0.5 score with a warning log.
Unit tests for scoring, diverse selection, exhibit formatting, and edge cases.

deliverables.description: Core curation module with ScoredCandidate, CurationRequest, CurationResult models, fetch_record_metadata(), score_candidates(), select_diverse(), and _fallback_significance_score().
Added recommendation_count and recommendation_criteria fields to ExplorationRequestLLM and ExplorationRequest. Updated system prompt RECOMMENDATION section with 5 examples.
Replaced static RECOMMENDATION stub with working handler that calls curator functions and returns exhibit-formatted response.
25 tests covering models, scoring, diverse selection, formatting, edge cases, and integration.

result.goal: Build a curation engine (scripts/chat/curator.py) that scores, diversely selects, and formats exhibit-quality subsets from large CandidateSets. Replace the static RECOMMENDATION stub in main.py with a working handler that parses curation requests ('select 10 for exhibit', 'most important items', 'representative sample'), scores candidates on significance, selects a diverse subset across date/place/language/agent dimensions, and returns exhibit-formatted responses with significance notes.

implementationSteps.description: Create the new module with Pydantic models and function signatures.
details: Define ScoredCandidate(BaseModel) with fields: record_id (str), significance_score (float, 0.0-1.0), reasons (List[str]), metadata (Dict[str, Any]) for dimension values used in diversity selection.
Define CurationRequest(BaseModel) with fields: n (int, default=10), criteria (Optional[str]), dimensions (List[str], default=['date','place','language','agent']).
Define CurationResult(BaseModel) with fields: selected (List[ScoredCandidate]), total_scored (int), selection_method (str), dimension_coverage (Dict[str, List[str]]).
Import significance_score from scripts.chat.thematic_context with try/except ImportError fallback.
Fallback stub: _fallback_significance_score(record_metadata: dict) -> float returns 0.5 and logs warning that E4 thematic_context is not yet available.
implementationSteps.title: Create ScoredCandidate model and curator.py skeleton

implementationSteps.description: SQL helper that fetches all metadata needed for scoring from bibliographic.db for a batch of record IDs.
details: Takes record_ids (List[str]) and db_path (Path) as arguments.
Single query joins records, imprints, languages, agents, subjects, and authority_enrichment tables.

Returns Dict[str, dict] mapping mms_id -> {date_start, date_end, place_norm, country_name, publisher_norm, languages: List[str], agents: List[str], subjects: List[str], has_enrichment: bool, enrichment_count: int}.
Uses parameterized SQL with placeholders for safety.
Batches queries if record_ids > 500 to avoid SQLite variable limits.
implementationSteps.title: Implement fetch_record_metadata() helper

implementationSteps.description: Score all candidates in a subgroup using significance_score() from E4 (or fallback).
details: Function signature: score_candidates(record_ids: List[str], db_path: Path) -> List[ScoredCandidate].
Calls fetch_record_metadata() to get metadata for all records.
For each record, calls significance_score(metadata) from thematic_context.py (E4 dependency).

Populates ScoredCandidate.reasons with human-readable explanations: 'Early printing (1475)', 'Rich enrichment data (3 Wikidata links)', 'Rare place of publication (Kraków)', 'Multiple subject headings (4 subjects)'.
Returns list sorted by significance_score descending.

If significance_score is unavailable (E4 not implemented), uses fallback that assigns: 0.1 for date_rarity (earlier = higher), 0.1 for enrichment presence, 0.05 for non-null place, 0.05 for having subjects. This ensures basic ordering even without E4.
implementationSteps.title: Implement score_candidates()

details: Function signature: select_diverse(scored: List[ScoredCandidate], n: int = 10, dimensions: List[str] = None) -> CurationResult.
Default dimensions: ['date_decade', 'place_norm', 'language', 'agent'].
Algorithm (greedy with diversity bonus):
1. Sort scored list by significance_score descending.
2. Pick the highest-scoring candidate as the first selection.
3. For each subsequent slot: iterate remaining candidates, compute a diversity bonus for each based on how many NEW dimension values it would add to the selection.
4. Diversity bonus formula: bonus = 0.15 * (new_dimension_values / total_dimensions). A candidate adding a new decade AND a new place gets bonus = 0.15 * 2/4 = 0.075.
5. Pick candidate with highest (significance_score + diversity_bonus).
6. Track selected dimension values in a dict: {dimension: set(values_already_selected)}.
7. Continue until n items selected or candidates exhausted.
Returns CurationResult with selected items, total_scored count, selection_method='greedy_diverse', and dimension_coverage showing which values were covered.
Edge case: if n >= len(scored), return all scored items sorted by score (no diversity filtering needed).
Edge case: if scored is empty, return empty CurationResult.
implementationSteps.title: Implement select_diverse()

details: Add format_exhibit_item(scored: ScoredCandidate, index: int, candidate: Candidate) -> str.
Format: 'Item {index} ({date_start}, {place_norm}): {title} -- {significance_note}'.
significance_note is the top reason from scored.reasons, or 'Selected for diversity' if only diversity contributed.
Add format_exhibit_response(curation_result: CurationResult, query_context: str) -> str.
Includes header: 'Curated selection of {n} items from {total} in this collection.'
Includes dimension coverage summary: 'Coverage: 5 decades, 4 places, 3 languages, 6 agents.'
Lists each item using format_exhibit_item().
Footer: 'Items selected for significance and diversity across date, place, language, and agent dimensions.'
implementationSteps.title: Add exhibit formatting to formatter.py

implementationSteps.description: Extend the LLM schema to parse curation parameters from natural language.
details: Add recommendation_count field to ExplorationRequestLLM: Optional[int] = Field(None, description='Number of items to select for exhibit/recommendation (e.g., 10, 5, 20)'). Default extraction: 10.
Add recommendation_criteria field to ExplorationRequestLLM: Optional[str] = Field(None, description='Selection criteria: exhibit, representative, important, significant, rare, diverse').
Add same fields to ExplorationRequest dataclass.
Update EXPLORATION_AGENT_SYSTEM_PROMPT RECOMMENDATION section with richer description and examples:
- 'select 10 for an exhibit' -> intent=recommendation, recommendation_count=10, recommendation_criteria='exhibit'
- 'most important items' -> intent=recommendation, recommendation_count=10, recommendation_criteria='important'
- 'representative sample of 20' -> intent=recommendation, recommendation_count=20, recommendation_criteria='representative'
- 'pick the rarest books' -> intent=recommendation, recommendation_count=10, recommendation_criteria='rare'
- 'diverse selection' -> intent=recommendation, recommendation_count=10, recommendation_criteria='diverse'
implementationSteps.title: Add recommendation fields to ExplorationRequestLLM

implementationSteps.description: Replace the static stub with working curation logic.
details: Import from scripts.chat.curator: score_candidates, select_diverse, CurationRequest.
Import from scripts.chat.formatter: format_exhibit_response.
Replace lines 1025-1038 (the static RECOMMENDATION handler) with:
1. Extract recommendation_count from exploration_request (default 10, clamp to 1-50 range).
2. Extract recommendation_criteria from exploration_request (default 'exhibit').
3. Call score_candidates(active_subgroup.record_ids, bib_db).
4. Call select_diverse(scored, n=recommendation_count).
5. Call format_exhibit_response(curation_result, active_subgroup.defining_query).

6. Build ChatResponse with: message=formatted_exhibit, metadata={'curation': {'total_scored': ..., 'selected_count': ..., 'dimension_coverage': ...}}, suggested_followups=['Show the full list', 'Select more items', 'Focus on a specific century', 'Show only items from {top_place}'].
7. Followup suggestions should be context-aware based on dimension_coverage.
implementationSteps.title: Wire up RECOMMENDATION handler in main.py

implementationSteps.description: TDD tests written before implementation (steps 1-4), plus integration tests.
details: Test score_candidates with mock DB: verify scores are floats in [0, 1], verify sorting descending, verify reasons are non-empty strings.
Test select_diverse with synthetic scored list: 20 items with known dimension values. Verify n items returned, verify dimension coverage is broader than top-n-by-score alone.
Test select_diverse edge cases: n > len(scored), n=0, n=1, empty scored list.
Test diversity algorithm: construct a case where top-3-by-score are all from same place/decade. Verify select_diverse(n=3) picks at least 2 different places.
Test format_exhibit_item: verify output contains date, place, title, and significance note.
Test format_exhibit_response: verify header, items, footer, and dimension coverage summary.
Test fallback scoring when thematic_context is not available.
Integration test (marked @pytest.mark.integration): run full pipeline against real bibliographic.db with a known subgroup.
implementationSteps.title: Write comprehensive tests

qualityGates.check: ruff check scripts/chat/curator.py scripts/chat/formatter.py scripts/chat/exploration_agent.py app/api/main.py
All public functions have full type hints including return types
The test_select_diverse_improves_coverage test passes: given 10 items where top-3-by-score are all from Rome/1500s, select_diverse(n=3) produces at least 2 distinct places.

Manual test: run a query returning 120 records, ask 'select 10 for exhibit', verify response contains 10 formatted items with significance notes and dimension coverage spanning at least 3 decades and 3 places.
test_score_candidates_fallback_scoring passes: when thematic_context is not importable, curator still produces non-degenerate scores.
score_candidates + select_diverse for 200 records completes in <1 second (wall clock).

reportFailuresAddressed.description: Curated exhibit: 120 records returned, no selection/ranking capability
reportFailuresAddressed.how_fixed: Curation engine scores all 120 records, selects a diverse top-N subset (default 10), and formats them as exhibit items with significance notes. The user can request specific N values ('select 5', 'pick 15'). Exhibit formatting includes: item number, date, place, title, and a significance note explaining why this item was chosen.

reportFailuresAddressed.description: Large result sets lack representative sampling
reportFailuresAddressed.how_fixed: The select_diverse() function can be invoked whenever a large result set needs a representative subset, providing automatic sampling across key bibliographic dimensions.

reportFailuresAddressed.description: Large result sets lack representative sampling
reportFailuresAddressed.how_fixed: Same mechanism as Q4 -- diverse selection provides representative cross-section.

reportFailuresAddressed.description: Large result sets lack representative sampling
reportFailuresAddressed.how_fixed: Same mechanism as Q4 -- diverse selection provides representative cross-section.

reportFailuresAddressed.description: Large result sets lack representative sampling
reportFailuresAddressed.how_fixed: Same mechanism as Q4 -- diverse selection provides representative cross-section.

retrievalChanges.description: Batch fetch of metadata for scoring. Joins records, imprints, languages, agents, subjects, authority_enrichment.
retrievalChanges.performance_note: For 120 records (Q20 case), this is a single query returning ~500 rows after joins. Aggregation into per-record metadata dicts happens in Python. Well within acceptable latency (<100ms with existing indexes).
retrievalChanges.sql_sketch: SELECT r.mms_id, i.date_start, i.date_end, i.place_norm, i.country_name, i.publisher_norm, l.code AS language, a.agent_norm, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM subjects s WHERE s.record_id = r.id) AS subject_count, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM authority_enrichment ae JOIN agents ag ON ag.authority_uri = ae.authority_uri WHERE ag.record_id = r.id) AS enrichment_count FROM records r LEFT JOIN imprints i ON i.record_id = r.id LEFT JOIN languages l ON l.record_id = r.id LEFT JOIN agents a ON a.record_id = r.id WHERE r.mms_id IN ({placeholders})

retrievalChanges.description: The existing handler at lines 1025-1038 returns a static message. The new handler calls curator.score_candidates() and curator.select_diverse(), which internally run the fetch_record_metadata SQL query. No changes to existing SQL queries or indexes.

risksAndEdgeCases.mitigation: curator.py includes a try/except ImportError for significance_score from thematic_context.py. Fallback stub (_fallback_significance_score) uses basic heuristics: date antiquity (earlier = higher), enrichment presence, place completeness, subject count. This ensures E5 is functional even before E4 is delivered. The fallback logs a warning so we know when it is active. When E4 lands, the fallback is automatically bypassed.
risksAndEdgeCases.test_strategy: Test both paths: mock thematic_context import success AND failure. Verify fallback produces non-degenerate scores (not all 0.5).

risksAndEdgeCases.mitigation: If n >= total records, select_diverse returns all records sorted by score with selection_method='top_n'. format_exhibit_response adjusts header: 'Showing all {n} items (collection has only {total}).' No diversity algorithm needed.
risksAndEdgeCases.test_strategy: Explicit test case with 2 records, n=10.

risksAndEdgeCases.mitigation: fetch_record_metadata batches SQL queries in groups of 500 to avoid SQLite SQLITE_MAX_VARIABLE_NUMBER limit (default 999). score_candidates processes all records but the greedy selection algorithm only iterates n * total times, which is O(n * N) -- for n=10 and N=1000, that is 10,000 iterations, negligible. Total latency should be <500ms.
risksAndEdgeCases.test_strategy: Benchmark test with 1000 synthetic scored items to verify select_diverse completes in <100ms.

risksAndEdgeCases.mitigation: Score factors degrade gracefully: if date_start is NULL, date_rarity contributes 0. If all fields are NULL, the record gets a minimal score (near 0.0) and is unlikely to be selected unless the pool is very sparse. Records with no metadata get reasons=['Limited metadata available'].
risksAndEdgeCases.risk: Missing metadata for scoring (NULL date_start, NULL place_norm)
risksAndEdgeCases.test_strategy: Test with records that have all-NULL metadata. Verify they score low but do not crash.

risksAndEdgeCases.mitigation: If no new dimension values can be added (diversity bonus is always 0), the algorithm degrades to pure top-N-by-score, which is still a valid and useful selection. selection_method is set to 'top_n_fallback' in this case.
risksAndEdgeCases.risk: Diversity algorithm degeneracy (all candidates identical on all dimensions)
risksAndEdgeCases.test_strategy: Test with 20 items all having identical date_decade, place, language, and agent.

risksAndEdgeCases.mitigation: Step 6 updates the system prompt with 5 explicit RECOMMENDATION examples covering the key phrasings: 'select N for exhibit', 'most important', 'representative sample', 'rarest books', 'diverse selection'. The prompt explicitly distinguishes RECOMMENDATION from AGGREGATION: 'RECOMMENDATION selects specific items; AGGREGATION counts/groups them.'
risksAndEdgeCases.risk: LLM misclassifies curation request as AGGREGATION or METADATA_QUESTION
risksAndEdgeCases.test_strategy: Add 5 exploration agent classification tests with curation-style inputs, verifying intent=RECOMMENDATION.

risksAndEdgeCases.mitigation: Handler clamps recommendation_count to [1, 50] range. If LLM returns None, default to 10. If user asks for 'all', set to min(50, total_records).
risksAndEdgeCases.test_strategy: Test with recommendation_count=0, 100, None, and negative values.

fields.description: Number of items to select for exhibit/recommendation (e.g., 10, 5, 20)
fields.name: recommendation_count

fields.description: Selection criteria: exhibit, representative, important, significant, rare, diverse
fields.name: recommendation_criteria

fields.description: MMS ID of the scored record
fields.name: record_id

fields.description: Human-readable reasons for the score, e.g., 'Early printing (1475)', 'Rich enrichment data'
fields.name: reasons

fields.description: Dimension values used for diversity selection: {date_decade: '1470s', place_norm: 'rome', language: 'lat', agent: 'aldus manutius'}
fields.name: metadata

fields.description: Total number of candidates that were scored
fields.name: total_scored

schemaChanges.note: metadata is already Dict[str, Any]. Curation responses will populate metadata['curation'] with: total_scored, selected_count, dimension_coverage, selection_method.

tddPlan.description: Tests are written BEFORE implementation for core logic (steps 1-4). Formatter and integration tests are written alongside implementation.

tests: test_scored_candidate_valid: score 0.5, 2 reasons, valid metadata dict
test_scored_candidate_score_bounds: score < 0 raises ValidationError, score > 1 raises ValidationError
test_scored_candidate_empty_reasons: empty reasons list is allowed (metadata-poor record)
test_fetch_metadata_returns_all_fields: in-memory SQLite with 3 records, verify dict keys
test_fetch_metadata_missing_record: mms_id not in DB returns empty dict for that ID
test_fetch_metadata_null_fields: record with NULL date_start still returns entry with date_start=None
test_fetch_metadata_batching: 600 record IDs trigger 2 batches (500 + 100)
test_score_candidates_sorted_descending: 5 records with known metadata, verify output order
test_score_candidates_reasons_populated: each ScoredCandidate has at least 1 reason string
test_score_candidates_empty_input: empty record_ids returns empty list
test_score_candidates_fallback_scoring: mock ImportError on thematic_context, verify fallback produces varied scores
test_select_diverse_basic: 20 items, n=5, verify 5 returned
test_select_diverse_improves_coverage: construct 10 items where top-3-by-score are all from Rome/1500s. Verify select_diverse(n=3) covers at least 2 distinct places.
test_select_diverse_n_exceeds_total: 5 items, n=10, verify all 5 returned, selection_method='top_n'
test_select_diverse_empty_input: empty list returns empty CurationResult
test_select_diverse_single_item: 1 item, n=5, returns 1 item
test_select_diverse_dimension_coverage_populated: verify dimension_coverage dict has entries for each requested dimension
test_select_diverse_identical_dimensions: all items same dimensions, verify degrades to top-N
test_format_exhibit_item_complete: verify output contains 'Item 1', date, place, title, significance note
test_format_exhibit_item_missing_date: verify graceful handling when date_start is None
test_format_exhibit_response_header: verify 'Curated selection of N items from M'
test_format_exhibit_response_coverage_summary: verify dimension counts in output
test_exploration_request_recommendation_fields: ExplorationRequestLLM accepts recommendation_count and recommendation_criteria
test_exploration_request_recommendation_defaults: recommendation_count defaults to None, recommendation_criteria defaults to None
test_recommendation_handler_returns_exhibit: mock exploration_request with intent=RECOMMENDATION, verify response.message contains 'Curated selection'
test_recommendation_handler_metadata: verify response.metadata['curation'] contains total_scored and selected_count
test_recommendation_handler_followups: verify suggested_followups are non-empty and context-relevant

implementationSteps.description: Extend the LLM schema to parse curation parameters from natural language.
details: Add recommendation_count field to ExplorationRequestLLM: Optional[int] = Field(None, description='Number of items to select for exhibit/recommendation (e.g., 10, 5, 20)'). Default extraction: 10.
Add recommendation_criteria field to ExplorationRequestLLM: Optional[str] = Field(None, description='Selection criteria: exhibit, representative, important, significant, rare, diverse').
Add same fields to ExplorationRequest dataclass.
Update EXPLORATION_AGENT_SYSTEM_PROMPT RECOMMENDATION section with richer description and examples:
- 'select 10 for an exhibit' -> intent=recommendation, recommendation_count=10, recommendation_criteria='exhibit'
- 'most important items' -> intent=recommendation, recommendation_count=10, recommendation_criteria='important'
- 'representative sample of 20' -> intent=recommendation, recommendation_count=20, recommendation_criteria='representative'
- 'pick the rarest books' -> intent=recommendation, recommendation_count=10, recommendation_criteria='rare'
- 'diverse selection' -> intent=recommendation, recommendation_count=10, recommendation_criteria='diverse'
implementationSteps.title: Add recommendation fields to ExplorationRequestLLM

implementationSteps.description: Replace the static stub with working curation logic.
details: Import from scripts.chat.curator: score_candidates, select_diverse, CurationRequest.
Import from scripts.chat.formatter: format_exhibit_response.
Replace lines 1025-1038 (the static RECOMMENDATION handler) with:
1. Extract recommendation_count from exploration_request (default 10, clamp to 1-50 range).
2. Extract recommendation_criteria from exploration_request (default 'exhibit').
3. Call score_candidates(active_subgroup.record_ids, bib_db).
4. Call select_diverse(scored, n=recommendation_count).
5. Call format_exhibit_response(curation_result, active_subgroup.defining_query).

6. Build ChatResponse with: message=formatted_exhibit, metadata={'curation': {'total_scored': ..., 'selected_count': ..., 'dimension_coverage': ...}}, suggested_followups=['Show the full list', 'Select more items', 'Focus on a specific century', 'Show only items from {top_place}'].
7. Followup suggestions should be context-aware based on dimension_coverage.
implementationSteps.title: Wire up RECOMMENDATION handler in main.py

implementationSteps.description: TDD tests written before implementation (steps 1-4), plus integration tests.
details: Test score_candidates with mock DB: verify scores are floats in [0, 1], verify sorting descending, verify reasons are non-empty strings.
Test select_diverse with synthetic scored list: 20 items with known dimension values. Verify n items returned, verify dimension coverage is broader than top-n-by-score alone.
Test select_diverse edge cases: n > len(scored), n=0, n=1, empty scored list.
Test diversity algorithm: construct a case where top-3-by-score are all from same place/decade. Verify select_diverse(n=3) picks at least 2 different places.
Test format_exhibit_item: verify output contains date, place, title, and significance note.
Test format_exhibit_response: verify header, items, footer, and dimension coverage summary.
Test fallback scoring when thematic_context is not available.
Integration test (marked @pytest.mark.integration): run full pipeline against real bibliographic.db with a known subgroup.
implementationSteps.title: Write comprehensive tests

qualityGates.check: ruff check scripts/chat/curator.py scripts/chat/formatter.py scripts/chat/exploration_agent.py app/api/main.py
All public functions have full type hints including return types
The test_select_diverse_improves_coverage test passes: given 10 items where top-3-by-score are all from Rome/1500s, select_diverse(n=3) produces at least 2 distinct places.

Manual test: run a query returning 120 records, ask 'select 10 for exhibit', verify response contains 10 formatted items with significance notes and dimension coverage spanning at least 3 decades and 3 places.
test_score_candidates_fallback_scoring passes: when thematic_context is not importable, curator still produces non-degenerate scores.
score_candidates + select_diverse for 200 records completes in <1 second (wall clock).

reportFailuresAddressed.description: Curated exhibit: 120 records returned, no selection/ranking capability
reportFailuresAddressed.how_fixed: Curation engine scores all 120 records, selects a diverse top-N subset (default 10), and formats them as exhibit items with significance notes. The user can request specific N values ('select 5', 'pick 15'). Exhibit formatting includes: item number, date, place, title, and a significance note explaining why this item was chosen.

reportFailuresAddressed.description: Large result sets lack representative sampling
reportFailuresAddressed.how_fixed: The select_diverse() function can be invoked whenever a large result set needs a representative subset, providing automatic sampling across key bibliographic dimensions.

reportFailuresAddressed.description: Large result sets lack representative sampling
reportFailuresAddressed.how_fixed: Same mechanism as Q4 -- diverse selection provides representative cross-section.

reportFailuresAddressed.description: Large result sets lack representative sampling
reportFailuresAddressed.how_fixed: Same mechanism as Q4 -- diverse selection provides representative cross-section.

reportFailuresAddressed.description: Large result sets lack representative sampling
reportFailuresAddressed.how_fixed: Same mechanism as Q4 -- diverse selection provides representative cross-section.

retrievalChanges.description: Batch fetch of metadata for scoring. Joins records, imprints, languages, agents, subjects, authority_enrichment.
retrievalChanges.performance_note: For 120 records (Q20 case), this is a single query returning ~500 rows after joins. Aggregation into per-record metadata dicts happens in Python. Well within acceptable latency (<100ms with existing indexes).
retrievalChanges.sql_sketch: SELECT r.mms_id, i.date_start, i.date_end, i.place_norm, i.country_name, i.publisher_norm, l.code AS language, a.agent_norm, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM subjects s WHERE s.record_id = r.id) AS subject_count, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM authority_enrichment ae JOIN agents ag ON ag.authority_uri = ae.authority_uri WHERE ag.record_id = r.id) AS enrichment_count FROM records r LEFT JOIN imprints i ON i.record_id = r.id LEFT JOIN languages l ON l.record_id = r.id LEFT JOIN agents a ON a.record_id = r.id WHERE r.mms_id IN ({placeholders})

retrievalChanges.description: The existing handler at lines 1025-1038 returns a static message. The new handler calls curator.score_candidates() and curator.select_diverse(), which internally run the fetch_record_metadata SQL query. No changes to existing SQL queries or indexes.

risksAndEdgeCases.mitigation: curator.py includes a try/except ImportError for significance_score from thematic_context.py. Fallback stub (_fallback_significance_score) uses basic heuristics: date antiquity (earlier = higher), enrichment presence, place completeness, subject count. This ensures E5 is functional even before E4 is delivered. The fallback logs a warning so we know when it is active. When E4 lands, the fallback is automatically bypassed.
risksAndEdgeCases.test_strategy: Test both paths: mock thematic_context import success AND failure. Verify fallback produces non-degenerate scores (not all 0.5).

risksAndEdgeCases.mitigation: If n >= total records, select_diverse returns all records sorted by score with selection_method='top_n'. format_exhibit_response adjusts header: 'Showing all {n} items (collection has only {total}).' No diversity algorithm needed.
risksAndEdgeCases.test_strategy: Explicit test case with 2 records, n=10.

risksAndEdgeCases.mitigation: fetch_record_metadata batches SQL queries in groups of 500 to avoid SQLite SQLITE_MAX_VARIABLE_NUMBER limit (default 999). score_candidates processes all records but the greedy selection algorithm only iterates n * total times, which is O(n * N) -- for n=10 and N=1000, that is 10,000 iterations, negligible. Total latency should be <500ms.
risksAndEdgeCases.test_strategy: Benchmark test with 1000 synthetic scored items to verify select_diverse completes in <100ms.

risksAndEdgeCases.mitigation: Score factors degrade gracefully: if date_start is NULL, date_rarity contributes 0. If all fields are NULL, the record gets a minimal score (near 0.0) and is unlikely to be selected unless the pool is very sparse. Records with no metadata get reasons=['Limited metadata available'].
risksAndEdgeCases.risk: Missing metadata for scoring (NULL date_start, NULL place_norm)
risksAndEdgeCases.test_strategy: Test with records that have all-NULL metadata. Verify they score low but do not crash.

risksAndEdgeCases.mitigation: If no new dimension values can be added (diversity bonus is always 0), the algorithm degrades to pure top-N-by-score, which is still a valid and useful selection. selection_method is set to 'top_n_fallback' in this case.
risksAndEdgeCases.risk: Diversity algorithm degeneracy (all candidates identical on all dimensions)
risksAndEdgeCases.test_strategy: Test with 20 items all having identical date_decade, place, language, and agent.

risksAndEdgeCases.mitigation: Step 6 updates the system prompt with 5 explicit RECOMMENDATION examples covering the key phrasings: 'select N for exhibit', 'most important', 'representative sample', 'rarest books', 'diverse selection'. The prompt explicitly distinguishes RECOMMENDATION from AGGREGATION: 'RECOMMENDATION selects specific items; AGGREGATION counts/groups them.'
risksAndEdgeCases.risk: LLM misclassifies curation request as AGGREGATION or METADATA_QUESTION
risksAndEdgeCases.test_strategy: Add 5 exploration agent classification tests with curation-style inputs, verifying intent=RECOMMENDATION.

risksAndEdgeCases.mitigation: Handler clamps recommendation_count to [1, 50] range. If LLM returns None, default to 10. If user asks for 'all', set to min(50, total_records).
risksAndEdgeCases.test_strategy: Test with recommendation_count=0, 100, None, and negative values.

fields.description: Number of items to select for exhibit/recommendation (e.g., 10, 5, 20)
fields.name: recommendation_count

fields.description: Selection criteria: exhibit, representative, important, significant, rare, diverse
fields.name: recommendation_criteria

fields.description: MMS ID of the scored record
fields.name: record_id

fields.description: Human-readable reasons for the score, e.g., 'Early printing (1475)', 'Rich enrichment data'
fields.name: reasons

fields.description: Dimension values used for diversity selection: {date_decade: '1470s', place_norm: 'rome', language: 'lat', agent: 'aldus manutius'}
fields.name: metadata

fields.description: Total number of candidates that were scored
fields.name: total_scored

schemaChanges.note: metadata is already Dict[str, Any]. Curation responses will populate metadata['curation'] with: total_scored, selected_count, dimension_coverage, selection_method.

tddPlan.description: Tests are written BEFORE implementation for core logic (steps 1-4). Formatter and integration tests are written alongside implementation.

tests: test_scored_candidate_valid: score 0.5, 2 reasons, valid metadata dict
test_scored_candidate_score_bounds: score < 0 raises ValidationError, score > 1 raises ValidationError
test_scored_candidate_empty_reasons: empty reasons list is allowed (metadata-poor record)
test_fetch_metadata_returns_all_fields: in-memory SQLite with 3 records, verify dict keys
test_fetch_metadata_missing_record: mms_id not in DB returns empty dict for that ID
test_fetch_metadata_null_fields: record with NULL date_start still returns entry with date_start=None
test_fetch_metadata_batching: 600 record IDs trigger 2 batches (500 + 100)
test_score_candidates_sorted_descending: 5 records with known metadata, verify output order
test_score_candidates_reasons_populated: each ScoredCandidate has at least 1 reason string
test_score_candidates_empty_input: empty record_ids returns empty list
test_score_candidates_fallback_scoring: mock ImportError on thematic_context, verify fallback produces varied scores
test_select_diverse_basic: 20 items, n=5, verify 5 returned
test_select_diverse_improves_coverage: construct 10 items where top-3-by-score are all from Rome/1500s. Verify select_diverse(n=3) covers at least 2 distinct places.
test_select_diverse_n_exceeds_total: 5 items, n=10, verify all 5 returned, selection_method='top_n'
test_select_diverse_empty_input: empty list returns empty CurationResult
test_select_diverse_single_item: 1 item, n=5, returns 1 item
test_select_diverse_dimension_coverage_populated: verify dimension_coverage dict has entries for each requested dimension
test_select_diverse_identical_dimensions: all items same dimensions, verify degrades to top-N
test_format_exhibit_item_complete: verify output contains 'Item 1', date, place, title, significance note
test_format_exhibit_item_missing_date: verify graceful handling when date_start is None
test_format_exhibit_response_header: verify 'Curated selection of N items from M'
test_format_exhibit_response_coverage_summary: verify dimension counts in output
test_exploration_request_recommendation_fields: ExplorationRequestLLM accepts recommendation_count and recommendation_criteria
test_exploration_request_recommendation_defaults: recommendation_count defaults to None, recommendation_criteria defaults to None
test_recommendation_handler_returns_exhibit: mock exploration_request with intent=RECOMMENDATION, verify response.message contains 'Curated selection'
test_recommendation_handler_metadata: verify response.metadata['curation'] contains total_scored and selected_count
test_recommendation_handler_followups: verify suggested_followups are non-empty and context-relevant
